summary: Reused the live StackOverflow-derived production-reliability asset inside the repo's unattended-workflow guide, added concrete guardrails, and repaired an outdated Start Here link so high-intent evaluators have a cleaner Codeberg-first path.

changes.change: Added a production-reliability guardrails section based on the strongest StackOverflow reliability answer spine.
Linked the deeper Ralph Site production-reliability article and fixed the outdated START_HERE_RALPHWORKFLOW.md link to START_HERE.md.

why_this_action: Codeberg adoption is still flat, so the best available move is improving repo-surface conversion instead of piling onto already-saturated outreach windows.
The StackOverflow reliability lane already produced a stronger reusable explanation earlier today, so reusing that asset is higher leverage than drafting another fresh packet.
This guide already serves high-intent evaluators asking whether unattended AI coding can be made trustworthy, so adding concrete guardrails reduces trust friction close to the Codeberg CTA.
verification: Confirmed the guide now includes production reliability guardrails
Confirmed the guide now links to ../../START_HERE.md instead of ../../START_HERE_RALPHWORKFLOW.md

expected_outcome.primary_goal: Increase trust and conversion for repo visitors who are evaluating unattended AI coding reliability before deciding whether to inspect or follow Codeberg.
expected_outcome.measurement_hint: Watch Codeberg stars/watchers/forks and any future docs-path engagement before adding more same-family outreach.
